title_en: Why is BHT allowed in the US?
title_es: ¿Por qué se permite BHT en EE.UU.?
hook_en: What if I told you your cereal has a banned additive?!
hook_es: ¿Y si te dijera que tu cereal tiene un aditivo prohibido?
key_message: BHT is an additive banned in the UK and EU but legal in the US, raising health concerns due to its potential as an endocrine disruptor.
visual_elements: comparison map of US vs. UK/EU regulations
short clip of actress reacting with surprise
_source_regulation_context:
ADDITIVES BANNED IN OTHER COUNTRIES BUT LEGAL IN US:
  • BHT (Butylated Hydroxytoluene): Cereals, snack foods, packaging materials — Banned in: UK, EU (restricted in food contact) — Health concern: Possible endocrine disruptor; linked to liver and kidney damage in animal studies

US OBESITY RATES OVER TIME:
  • 1960: 13.4% (adults), 5.0% (children)
  • 1980: 15.0% (adults), 5.5% (children)
  • 1994: 22.5% (adults), 10.0% (children)
  • 2000: 30.5% (adults), 13.9% (children)
  • 2010: 35.7% (adults), 16.9% (children)
  • 2016: 39.8% (adults), 18.5% (children)
  • 2020: 41.9% (adults), 19.7% (children)
  • 2024: 43.0% (adults), 21.0% (children)

title_en: The US Obesity Epidemic: Deregulation Impact
title_es: La Epidemia de Obesidad en EE.UU.: Impacto de la Desregulación
hook_en: US obesity rates have skyrocketed — what’s fueling it?
hook_es: Las tasas de obesidad en EE.UU. se han disparado, ¿qué lo está causando?
key_message: Deregulation in food safety has contributed to the US obesity epidemic, as seen in skyrocketing obesity rates over the decades.
visual_elements: graph showing US obesity rates from 1960 to 2024
clip of actress pointing to increasing line on graph
